touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Tyler Nguyen at AspenDynamics. No answer, left voicemail introducing our AI sales intelligence platform and mentioning rep productivity benefits.
Initial cold email to Tyler Nguyen highlighting how sales teams in managed IT services are using AI to improve rep productivity. No response received.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Tyler Nguyen with personalized note about AspenDynamics' growth and sales team challenges. No response.
Follow-up LinkedIn message sharing case study from similar managed IT company that improved rep productivity by 30%. No response.
Second email attempt with different subject line focusing on conversation intelligence and coaching. No response received.
Email highlighting specific pain points for cybersecurity sales teams and offering 15-minute discovery call. No response.
LinkedIn message asking if Tyler had seen previous outreach and if timing was better now. Tyler responded positively, mentioning they're exploring sales enablement tools.

questions_asked.question: What makes your platform different from Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Brief response mentioning our consulting services and automation capabilities beyond just conversation intelligence.

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sharing calendar link for qualification call. No additional response needed.
Attempted to confirm call time via phone. Tyler unavailable, left voicemail confirming meeting details.
Pre-call reminder attempt. No answer, meeting still scheduled.
Email sent with agenda for upcoming qualification call and questions about current sales process. Tyler confirmed attendance and provided initial context about team size and current tools.

questions_asked.question: What tools is your sales team currently using?
questions_asked.answer: Salesforce CRM, some email tracking, but nothing for conversation intelligence yet.

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute qualification call with Tyler Nguyen. Discussed current sales team structure (68 reps across cybersecurity and managed IT), main pain points around rep onboarding and coaching consistency. Tyler mentioned evaluating Gong but concerned about implementation complexity. Confirmed budget authority sits with CRO Finn Rogers.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take?
questions_asked.answer: 4-6 weeks with our consulting package, includes custom workflow automation setup.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Looking to have something in place by end of quarter, about 8 weeks out.

objections: We're already talking to Gong and they seem to be the industry standard.
next_steps: Tyler to discuss internally with CRO
Schedule discovery call with broader team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email recapping qualification call, sharing ROI calculator and case study from similar-sized managed IT company. Tyler responded asking about pricing ballpark and availability for demo next week.

questions_asked.question: Can you give us a rough pricing estimate for 68 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Provided range of $95K-$120K annually depending on feature tier and consulting scope.

next_steps: Tyler to loop in Sales Managers

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sharing additional resource about sales coaching best practices. No response needed.

Discovery email sent with questions about specific use cases, current coaching workflow, and integration requirements. Tyler responded with detailed answers and confirmed demo attendees: himself, Riley Harris and Luna Ramirez (Sales Managers), and Isaac Costa (technical evaluator).

questions_asked.question: What are your must-have integration requirements?
questions_asked.answer: Salesforce is critical, Slack for notifications would be nice to have.

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo email with agenda, login instructions for sandbox, and questions to prepare for demo. Tyler confirmed all attendees and shared specific workflow they want to see demonstrated.

questions_asked.question: Can you show us how the automation works for deal risk identification?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed this will be part of the demo flow.

touches.ai_summary: 46-minute demo with Tyler, Luna, Riley, and Isaac. Walked through conversation intelligence, coaching workflows, and automation capabilities. Team engaged positively, particularly interested in risk calibration features. Isaac asked detailed questions about data security and API capabilities. Tyler mentioned Gong demo is scheduled for next week.

questions_asked.question: How does your AI model handle our industry-specific terminology?
questions_asked.answer: Model can be fine-tuned with custom vocabulary, included in consulting package.

questions_asked.question: What's the data retention policy?
questions_asked.answer: Configurable retention, default 2 years, can extend or reduce based on compliance needs.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for your team in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Tyler: faster rep ramp time and more consistent coaching across managers.

objections: The UI looks more complex than Gong's - concerned about user adoption.
next_steps: Tyler to schedule follow-up demo focused on specific workflows

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute follow-up demo focusing on custom automation workflows and simplified UI views. Tyler brought Finn Rogers (CRO) to this call. Finn engaged positively, asked about ROI metrics and implementation timeline. Discussion around consulting services differentiation from Gong.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ROI are similar customers seeing?
questions_asked.answer: Shared metrics: 25% faster rep ramp, 15% increase in win rates, 30% reduction in coaching time.

questions_asked.question: How hands-on is the consulting piece?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM for first 90 days, includes workflow design and change management support.

questions_asked.question: Have you completed the Gong demo yet?
questions_asked.answer: Finn: Yes, impressed by their platform but want to see if your consulting approach delivers more value.

next_steps: Tyler to gather requirements from IT for procurement
Schedule exec alignment call with Finn

touches.ai_summary: 41-minute executive alignment call with Finn Rogers and Tyler Nguyen. Discussed business case, implementation roadmap, and success metrics. Finn confirmed budget of $100K and expressed interest in moving forward pending final pricing and security review. Referenced Gong as main alternative but appreciated our consulting model.

questions_asked.question: Can you work within a $100K budget for 68 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, proposed automations plus consulting tier at $100K ACV.

questions_asked.question: What does the contract term look like?
questions_asked.answer: Standard 12-month agreement, quarterly business reviews included.

questions_asked.question: What's your decision-making process from here?
questions_asked.answer: Finn: Need security review from Anika in IT, then final pricing comparison with Gong.

objections: Gong's pricing came in lower for just the platform - need to justify the consulting premium.
next_steps: Connect Anika Richter with Isaac for security review

touches.ai_summary: Email with formal proposal document, pricing breakdown, implementation timeline, and SOW for consulting services. Tyler acknowledged receipt and said they're reviewing internally with Finn and Anika.
next_steps: Tyler to schedule pricing discussion after internal review

touches.ai_summary: Accidental duplicate outbound email sent to Tyler (prospecting sequence not paused). No response.
Follow-up email checking on proposal review and offering to answer questions. Tyler responded that they're comparing with Gong's final offer and Anika has questions about data processing agreements.

questions_asked.question: Can you provide the DPA template before the pricing call?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed, will send standard DPA and can customize as needed.

touches.ai_summary: Email sharing DPA, security documentation, and answers to Anika's compliance questions. Tyler confirmed pricing call scheduled for next day.

22-minute pricing call with Tyler. Discussed proposal details and compared value proposition with Gong. Tyler shared that Gong came in at $78K but without consulting. Negotiated consulting scope and pricing structure. Tyler comfortable with $100K but needs Finn's final approval.

questions_asked.question: Can you reduce the consulting hours to lower the price?
questions_asked.answer: Explained value of full consulting package, offered flexible payment terms instead.

questions_asked.question: Is the $22K difference compared to Gong a blocker for Finn?
questions_asked.answer: Tyler: No, if we can prove ROI on the consulting side. Finn values the hands-on support.

objections: Gong's platform-only price is significantly lower - need to make sure consulting ROI is clear.
next_steps: Tyler to present recommendation to Finn

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute executive call with Finn Rogers and Tyler. Reviewed final proposal, ROI justification for consulting premium over Gong, and implementation timeline. Finn expressed comfort with $100K investment and value proposition. Discussed contract terms and start date.

questions_asked.question: Can we start implementation in 3 weeks instead of 4?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed we can accommodate accelerated timeline.

questions_asked.question: Are there any other stakeholders who need to weigh in?
questions_asked.answer: Finn: Anika needs to sign off on security, but that should be complete this week.

touches.ai_summary: 8-minute pricing confirmation call with Tyler. Anika approved security terms with minor DPA modifications. Tyler confirmed Finn ready to move forward pending final contract redlines from legal.

questions_asked.question: Can you accept our standard liability cap language?
questions_asked.answer: Agreed to review, likely acceptable with standard modifications.

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute negotiation call with Tyler reviewing final contract terms. Discussed liability language, payment terms (quarterly vs annual), and renewal clauses. Tyler confirmed all stakeholders aligned and ready to sign pending final legal approval.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Agreed to quarterly payments with standard payment terms.

questions_asked.question: What's the timeline for legal to finalize?
questions_asked.answer: Tyler: Should have signed contract by end of this week.
